solution_steps.description: List all variables and relationships based on the clues provided in the riddle.
solution_steps.reasoning: The riddle involves five houses, each characterized by a color, nationality, beverage, cigarette brand, and pet. By organizing these variables, I can set up a logical framework to solve the problem.

solution_steps.description: Translate the clues into a logical grid format, creating a matrix to track relationships between different attributes of each house.
solution_steps.reasoning: This allows me to identify how each attribute (like color or nationality) interacts with others and helps in deducing the properties of each house.

solution_steps.description: Systematically apply the clues to fill in the matrix, eliminating impossibilities and confirming relationships until all houses are defined.
solution_steps.reasoning: Applying the clues iteratively allows me to deduce the positions of each attribute. For example, if one clue states that the Brit lives in the red house, I can place the Brit in the red house and use this information to eliminate other possibilities.

solution_steps.description: Identify the house which has the fish based on the clues and the filled matrix.
solution_steps.reasoning: Once all houses are fully defined, I can directly identify which nationality corresponds to the house that has the fish.

assumptions: All clues provided are consistent and complete without any contradictions.
The setup of the puzzle follows the classic structure of the Einstein riddle as commonly known.

solution_steps.description: Identify the clues and setup a grid for the puzzle.
solution_steps.reasoning: To solve the Einstein riddle, we need to organize the information systematically. There are five houses with various attributes, and we will create a grid to track the relationships between the different categories (color, nationality, beverage, brand, pet). The clues help in establishing these relationships.

solution_steps.description: Translate the clues into logical relationships.
solution_steps.reasoning: Each clue provides a piece of information that helps connect different attributes. For example, if a clue states that the Brit lives in the red house, we can use this relationship to fill in parts of our grid and eliminate impossibilities.

solution_steps.description: Incrementally fill the grid using the clues provided.
solution_steps.reasoning: By systematically applying each clue, I will fill in the grid with known information and deduce unknowns. This often involves using the process of elimination and logical deduction.

solution_steps.description: Identify the relationships that lead to the owner of the fish.
solution_steps.reasoning: As the grid fills out, I will be able to infer the relationships more clearly. The fish ownership will become evident through the process of elimination, where all other pets are assigned to their respective owners.

solution_steps.description: Confirm the final deduction about the fish's owner.
solution_steps.reasoning: After all clues have been accounted for and the grid filled, I will confirm the nationality associated with the fish by referencing the filled attributes in the grid.

assumptions: All clues are consistent and lead to a unique solution.

solution_steps.description: Sequentially apply each clue to fill in the grid, ruling out possibilities and confirming relationships.
